tooling_sandboxing.detail: Ships its own Docker-based [sandbox] execution mode (image, memory/cpu limits, network settings in config.toml) but it is opt-in and disabled by default (`#use_sandbox = false`, commented out), so tool execution runs on the host by default; a separate open issue proposes extending sandboxing to cover all tool operations by default.

context_memory.detail: Agents add results to an in-process memory/context object during a single interactive task loop; no database or file-based persistence across separate sessions is documented.

lifecycle_hooks.detail: Looked for a before/after tool-call callback or middleware system beyond the BaseAgent/ReActAgent/ToolCallAgent class hierarchy; found no documented interception API for blocking or tracing individual tool calls.

prompt_optimization.detail: Agent system_prompt/next_step_prompt were moved out of code and into config.toml so users can adjust agent behavior (e.g. Manus/PlanningAgent prompts) without code changes.

build_vs_buy.label: blueprint
build_vs_buy.detail: Self-hosted, MIT-licensed, config.toml-driven agent framework (conda/uv local install) positioned as an open alternative to invite-only Manus; only a HuggingFace Spaces demo exists, no managed hosted offering.
